commands.repair_reason: - [P1] The remaining blocker is maintainer/security judgment on the memory prompt boundary plus contributor real behavior proof, not a safe automated repair.; Needs attention: The diff changes the prompt authority of untrusted recalled memory without adding an enforced data-only or agent/session boundary.; Review finding: [P1] Do not promote recalled memories to system prompt — extensions/memory-lancedb/index.ts:1934 (sha=33c5732f21dc58ba3ee17a2dde7a52b7981d5f42)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes memory-lancedb auto-recall to return recalled memories through `prependSystemContext` instead of `prependContext`, with tests updated for that hook-result field move.

PR surface: Source 0, Tests +2. Total +2 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible: current main returns memory-lancedb auto-recall through `prependContext`, and both agent runner paths prepend that field to the model prompt. I did not run a live model scenario in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining blocker is maintainer/security judgment on the memory prompt boundary plus contributor real behavior proof, not a safe automated repair.

target.title: fix(memory): keep recalled memory out of user prompts
target.body:
## Summary

- Fixes #83437 by moving memory-lancedb auto-recall output from prompt `prependContext` to plugin `prependSystemContext`.
- Keeps recalled memories available as contextual data while avoiding prepending stored memory text to the current user prompt.
- Updates auto-recall regression tests to assert the user prompt is not rewritten and recalled memory is delivered through system-context hook output.

## Behavior or issue addressed

#83437 reports that relevant memories can be treated like fresh prompt instructions when auto-recall prepends them to the prompt text. This changes the auto-recall hook boundary so recalled memories are still marked untrusted, but are returned through plugin system context instead of `prependContext`.

## Real environment tested

Local OpenClaw repository worktree on branch `fix/issue-83437-memory-boundary`, using the repository-pinned package manager via `corepack pnpm@11.2.2`.

## Exact steps or command run after this patch

```bash
corepack pnpm@11.2.2 --dir /media/vdb/migrated-from-root-media-vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/issues-83437-92273 exec vitest run extensions/memory-lancedb/index.test.ts -t "runs auto-recall through the registered before_prompt_build hook|uses live runtime config to enable auto-recall after startup disable"
```

```bash
git -C /media/vdb/migrated-from-root-media-vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/issues-83437-92273 diff --check
```

## Evidence after fix

```text
Test Files  1 passed (1)
     Tests  2 passed | 123 skipped (125)
  Duration  6.82s
```

```text
git diff --check
# no output
```

## Observed result after fix

The auto-recall hook returns no `prependContext`; the recalled memory appears in `prependSystemContext` with the existing untrusted-memory marker.

## What was not tested

External gateway UI or live multi-agent runtime behavior was not exercised. The proof is a focused local regression test of the memory-lancedb hook contract plus whitespace validation.

## Risk

Low. The change is scoped to the memory-lancedb auto-recall hook return field and keeps the existing memory formatting, filtering, and untrusted marker unchanged.
target.author: mushuiyu886
